story.iWant: to configure provider chains per agent role, map workflow phases to roles, and set per-role prompts/tools/budgets
story.soThat: I can run multi-agent workflows with different AI providers per role while maintaining backward compatibility with the existing single-agent config

tasks.task: - [ ] Create `packages/shared/src/types/agent-config.ts` - [ ] Define `WorkflowPhase` union type with 8 phases - [ ] Define `DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP` constant mapping phases to `AgentType` - [ ] Define `ProviderChainEntry` interface (provider, model, apiKey, config) - [ ] Define `AgentRoleConfig` interface (providerChain, allowedTools, maxBudgetUsd, permissionMode, systemPrompt, providerPrompts) - [ ] Define `AgentsConfig` interface (defaults, roles, phaseRoleMap) - [ ] Define `SecurityConfig` interface (sanitizeContent, validateUrls, gateActions, maxFetchSizeBytes, blockedCommandPatterns) - [ ] Import `AgentType` from `./knowledge.js`

task.title: Extend TammaConfig with new optional fields

tasks.task: - [ ] Add `agents?: AgentsConfig` to `TammaConfig` in `packages/shared/src/types/index.ts` - [ ] Add `security?: SecurityConfig` to `TammaConfig` - [ ] Keep existing `agent: AgentConfig` as required field (backward compatibility) - [ ] Add import for `AgentsConfig` and `SecurityConfig` from `./agent-config.js`

task.title: Fix mergeConfig() to propagate optional fields

tasks.task: - [ ] Update `mergeConfig()` in `packages/cli/src/config.ts` to propagate `agents` - [ ] Propagate `security` field through merge - [ ] Propagate `aiProviders` field through merge - [ ] Propagate `defaultProvider` field through merge - [ ] Propagate `elsa` field through merge - [ ] Propagate `server` field through merge - [ ] Verify merge precedence: env > CLI > config file > defaults

task.title: Implement normalizeAgentsConfig() with legacy provider mapping

tasks.task: - [ ] Define `LEGACY_PROVIDER_MAP` constant mapping `AIProviderType` to provider chain names - [ ] Map `'anthropic'` to `'claude-code'` - [ ] Map `'openai'` to `'openrouter'` - [ ] Map `'local'` to `'openrouter'` - [ ] Implement `normalizeAgentsConfig(config: TammaConfig): AgentsConfig` - [ ] Return `config.agents` directly if already set - [ ] Fall back to deriving `AgentsConfig` from legacy `config.agent` field - [ ] Export function from `packages/cli/src/config.ts`

task.title: Add environment variable support for security config

tasks.task: - [ ] Add `TAMMA_SANITIZE_CONTENT` env var support (boolean) - [ ] Add `TAMMA_VALIDATE_URLS` env var support (boolean) - [ ] Add `TAMMA_GATE_ACTIONS` env var support (boolean) - [ ] Add `TAMMA_MAX_FETCH_SIZE_BYTES` env var support (integer) - [ ] Add `TAMMA_AGENT_PROVIDER` env var support (maps to `agent.provider`) - [ ] Update `loadEnvConfig()` in `packages/cli/src/config.ts` - [ ] Re-export new types from `packages/shared/src/index.ts`

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: New types file `packages/shared/src/types/agent-config.ts` compiles under strict TypeScript with WorkflowPhase, DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP, ProviderChainEntry, AgentRoleConfig, AgentsConfig, and SecurityConfig
`TammaConfig` in `packages/shared/src/types/index.ts` has `agents?: AgentsConfig` and `security?: SecurityConfig` while keeping `agent: AgentConfig` required
`mergeConfig()` in `packages/cli/src/config.ts` preserves all optional top-level fields (agents, security, aiProviders, defaultProvider, elsa, server) through config layering
`normalizeAgentsConfig()` returns `config.agents` when present; otherwise derives AgentsConfig from legacy `config.agent` using LEGACY_PROVIDER_MAP
`normalizeAgentsConfig()` maps `provider: 'anthropic'` to chain entry `{ provider: 'claude-code' }` and `provider: 'openai'` to `{ provider: 'openrouter' }`
Environment variable `TAMMA_AGENT_PROVIDER` sets `agent.provider` for legacy config path
All new types are re-exported from `packages/shared/src/index.ts`
`pnpm vitest run packages/shared/src/types/agent-config` passes with unit tests covering type exports, DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP, normalizeAgentsConfig, and env var parsing

docs.doc: Story specification with full type definitions and code snippets
Technical architecture (TammaConfig, event sourcing, naming conventions)
Project coding standards, TypeScript strict mode, naming conventions

code.file: TammaConfig interface -- add agents? and security? fields
AgentType union type used by AgentsConfig roles
New types file: WorkflowPhase, ProviderChainEntry, AgentRoleConfig, AgentsConfig, SecurityConfig
Unit tests for new types and normalizeAgentsConfig()

constraints.constraint: `agent: AgentConfig` must remain required in TammaConfig; existing configs with only `agent` must continue to work
All code must compile under TypeScript strict mode (strict: true, noImplicitAny, noImplicitReturns)
Files use kebab-case; interfaces use I-prefix; types use PascalCase; constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
Import AgentType from './knowledge.js' (not re-declare); use .js extensions in import paths for ESM
No API keys or secrets in default config values or test fixtures
normalizeAgentsConfig() must not mutate the input config object

interface.name: TammaConfig
interface.description: Root configuration interface with mode, logLevel, github, agent, engine, and optional fields

interface.name: AgentConfig
interface.description: Legacy single-agent config with model, provider?, maxBudgetUsd, allowedTools, permissionMode

standards.standard: 80% line coverage, 75% branch coverage, 85% function coverage
TDD: write tests before or alongside implementation
Mock external dependencies; no real API calls in unit tests

locations.location: Unit tests for type exports and DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP
Unit tests for mergeConfig(), normalizeAgentsConfig(), loadEnvConfig() changes (extend existing)

ideas.idea: Verify all 8 WorkflowPhase values are present in DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP
Verify DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP values are valid AgentType values
Test normalizeAgentsConfig() with only legacy `agent` field returns correct provider chain
Test normalizeAgentsConfig() with `agents` field returns it directly without modification
Test mergeConfig() preserves agents/security when only base has them
Test mergeConfig() overrides agents/security when override provides them
Test mergeConfig() does not inject undefined fields when neither base nor override has optional fields
Test loadEnvConfig() with TAMMA_SANITIZE_CONTENT=true sets securityConfig.sanitizeContent=true
Test loadEnvConfig() with TAMMA_SANITIZE_CONTENT=false sets securityConfig.sanitizeContent=false
Test loadEnvConfig() ignores invalid TAMMA_SANITIZE_CONTENT values (e.g., 'yes')
Test loadEnvConfig() with TAMMA_MAX_FETCH_SIZE_BYTES=1048576 parses to number
Test loadEnvConfig() with TAMMA_AGENT_PROVIDER=anthropic sets agent.provider
Test loadEnvConfig() ignores invalid TAMMA_AGENT_PROVIDER values
Test full config layering: defaults -> file config with agents -> env security overrides
Test example JSON config from story spec parses correctly as Partial<TammaConfig>
